status_detail: Orsted developing large-scale green hydrogen projects in Denmark. Includes the 100 MW H2RES project and Renewable Hydrogen project at Avedore Holme. PEM electrolysis powered by Orsted's offshore wind portfolio. Targeting industrial offtake for refining and chemicals.

data_management.narrative_summary: Orsted, Denmark's leading energy company, is developing large-scale green hydrogen projects powered by its extensive offshore wind portfolio. The H2RES project (100 MW PEM electrolysis) at Avedore Holme near Copenhagen is one of the most advanced, targeting industrial hydrogen offtake for refining and chemicals in the Copenhagen region. Orsted combines its offshore wind assets with downstream hydrogen offtake agreements to create integrated renewable hydrogen value chains. The company also participates in broader hydrogen projects including the Copenhagen Hydrogen Valley network. Orsted is one of the few renewable developers with a dedicated hydrogen business unit and a visible project pipeline.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected. Powered by Orsted offshore wind portfolio.
power.ppa_structure: Behind-the-meter from Orsted offshore wind assets

offtake.offtaker_name: Industrial users in Copenhagen region (refineries, chemical plants)

sources.title: Orsted H2RES — renewable hydrogen from offshore wind in Copenhagen
sources.author: Orsted A/S / IEA
